label: false-dup
a: JWTs are stored in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage. httpOnly prevents JavaScript from accessing the cookie, protecting against XSS attacks stealing the token.
b: Access tokens (JWTs) rotate every 15 minutes. The short lifetime limits exposure if a token is stolen or intercepted.
reviewer_notes: Both about JWT security but different mechanisms: A is cookie storage attributes for XSS protection; B is short token lifetime to bound theft. Different defences.
